name: Capetown Fruit & Vegetable Curry
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the peanut oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the onions and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they are golden brown.
2. Stir in the garlic, ginger, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, cinnamon, turmeric, cayenne, fennel seeds, cardamom, and cloves. Cook for an additional 3 minutes, ensuring the spices are well incorporated.
3. Add the zucchini and water to the pan. Stir well to prevent the spices from sticking to the pot. Cover and simmer on low heat for 10 minutes.
4. Mix in the green beans, apples, peppers, and dried apricots. Continue simmering gently, covered, for about 30 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add more water if needed to prevent sticking.
5. When the fruit and vegetables are tender, stir in the raisins, conserve, and lemon juice. Taste and adjust seasoning as desired. If you prefer a spicier dish, add more cayenne or garam masala. For a sweeter flavor, add more conserve. For a tangier taste, add more lemon juice.
6. Serve hot over a bed of rice, topped with nuts and banana slices.
